target.name: Phoenix Tailings
target.founder: Nick Myers (CEO), Tomás Villalón (CTO, Activate Boston fellow), Anthony Balladon

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $118M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Phoenix Tailings is a Series B climate and energy company led by Nick Myers and Tomás Villalón that has raised $117.8M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparison universe of nine private frontier-tech peers in the same sector, plus one public comparable, with direct stage-matched peers including KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy—all at Series C.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $118M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implying a valuation of $224M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M (Amogy) to $3.0B (KoBold Metals). The peer-median method carries material weight given the direct stage alignment and sector match, though the wide spread ($700M–$3.0B) reflects heterogeneity in capital deployment and commercial stage within this cohort.

Triangulation across both methods produces a low estimate of $190M, a median of $612M, and a high of $1.1B. The wide range reflects tension between the conservative stage-progression approach and the elevated peer median; key uncertainties include the company's traction relative to named peers, capital deployment runway, and technology readiness. The $612M median represents the central case absent material new data on operational milestones or fundraise terms.
